quiz.title: Unit 7 Quiz 2, Grade 11 Chemistry
quiz.subtitle: More Molar Mass Calculations
quiz.subject: Chemistry
quiz.topic: Molar Mass, Formula Mass, Atomic Mass, Calculating Molar Mass of Common Compounds

questions.question: The molar mass of phosphoric acid (H₃PO₄) is approximately:
questions.explanation: Phosphoric acid (H₃PO₄): H = 3 × 1.008 = 3.024 g/mol, P = 30.97 g/mol, O = 4 × 16.00 = 64.00 g/mol. Total = 97.99 g/mol ≈ 98.00 g/mol.

questions.question: The molar mass of benzene (C₆H₆) is approximately:
questions.explanation: Benzene (C₆H₆): C = 6 × 12.01 = 72.06 g/mol, H = 6 × 1.008 = 6.048 g/mol. Total = 78.11 g/mol.

questions.question: The molar mass of silver nitrate (AgNO₃) is approximately:
questions.explanation: Silver nitrate (AgNO₃): Ag = 107.87 g/mol, N = 14.01 g/mol, O = 3 × 16.00 = 48.00 g/mol. Total = 169.88 g/mol ≈ 169.87 g/mol.

questions.question: The molar mass of methane (CH₄) is approximately:
questions.explanation: Methane (CH₄): C = 12.01 g/mol, H = 4 × 1.008 = 4.032 g/mol. Total = 16.04 g/mol.

questions.question: The molar mass of barium chloride (BaCl₂) is approximately:
questions.explanation: Barium chloride (BaCl₂): Ba = 137.33 g/mol, Cl = 2 × 35.45 = 70.90 g/mol. Total = 208.23 g/mol.

questions.question: The molar mass of oxalic acid dihydrate (H₂C₂O₄·2H₂O) is approximately:
The molar mass of potassium dichromate (K₂Cr₂O₇) is approximately:
questions.explanation: Potassium dichromate (K₂Cr₂O₇): K = 2 × 39.10 = 78.20 g/mol, Cr = 2 × 52.00 = 104.00 g/mol, O = 7 × 16.00 = 112.00 g/mol. Total = 294.20 g/mol ≈ 294.18 g/mol.

questions.question: The molar mass of sucrose (C₁₂H₂₂O₁₁) is approximately:
The molar mass of calcium phosphate (Ca₃(PO₄)₂) is approximately:
questions.explanation: Calcium phosphate (Ca₃(PO₄)₂): Ca = 3 × 40.08 = 120.24 g/mol, P = 2 × 30.97 = 61.94 g/mol, O = 8 × 16.00 = 128.00 g/mol. Total = 310.18 g/mol.

questions.question: The molar mass of hydrogen peroxide (H₂O₂) is approximately:
questions.explanation: Hydrogen peroxide (H₂O₂): H = 2 × 1.008 = 2.016 g/mol, O = 2 × 16.00 = 32.00 g/mol. Total = 34.02 g/mol.

questions.question: The molar mass of aluminum sulfate (Al₂(SO₄)₃) is approximately:
questions.explanation: Aluminum sulfate (Al₂(SO₄)₃): Al = 2 × 26.98 = 53.96 g/mol, S = 3 × 32.06 = 96.18 g/mol, O = 12 × 16.00 = 192.00 g/mol. Total = 342.14 g/mol ≈ 342.15 g/mol.

questions.question: The molar mass of urea (CO(NH₂)₂) is approximately:
The molar mass of potassium chloride (KCl) is approximately:
questions.explanation: Potassium chloride (KCl): K = 39.10 g/mol, Cl = 35.45 g/mol. Total = 74.55 g/mol.

questions.question: The molar mass of aspirin (C₉H₈O₄) is approximately:
The molar mass of hydrated cobalt(II) chloride (CoCl₂·6H₂O) is approximately:
questions.explanation: Cobalt(II) chloride hexahydrate (CoCl₂·6H₂O): Co = 58.93 g/mol, Cl = 2 × 35.45 = 70.90 g/mol, 6H₂O = 6 × 18.02 = 108.12 g/mol. Total = 237.95 g/mol ≈ 237.93 g/mol.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered molar mass calculations for a variety of compounds! 🧪⚖️
scoreMessages.70: Good job! Review compounds with polyatomic ions and hydrated salts.
scoreMessages.50: Satisfactory. Focus on carefully counting atoms in complex formulas.
scoreMessages.0: Please review how to calculate molar mass by adding the atomic masses of all atoms in the formula.
